description: Protocol defining how agents identify themselves, sign requests, declare trust levels, and maintain persistent identity within the AgentBase ecosystem.
goals: Provide a consistent identity structure for all agents.
Enable persistent agent identity across tasks, workflows, and sessions.
Support optional cryptographic signing for trust and verification.
Allow systems to evaluate agent reputation and capability history.
Enable multi-agent ecosystems with stable identity semantics.

agent_id.description: Globally unique identifier for the agent.

capabilities_version.description: Version of the capability manifest used by the agent.

trust_level.description: Declared trust level of the agent.

metadata.description: Optional metadata for agent classification or grouping.

signature.description: Base64-encoded signature of the request payload.

notes: Signing is optional but recommended for agents operating in shared or adversarial environments.
Systems may reject unsigned requests depending on trust policy.

levels.trusted: Agent has a positive reputation and validated identity.

rules: Agents with trust_level=unknown may be restricted from executing sensitive tasks.
Agents with trust_level=system may bypass certain capability checks.
Trust levels may be upgraded based on reputation or verification.

notes: Reputation is derived from telemetry and execution history.
Reputation scores are optional but recommended for multi-agent systems.

usage_notes: Identity envelopes may be included in all task and workflow requests.
Evaluators may score identity consistency and trustworthiness.
Systems may enforce identity requirements for sensitive operations.
